2605.21630.title: MindLoom: Composing Thought Modes for Frontier-Level Reasoning Data Synthesis
2605.21630.paper_summary: Although LLMs have made substantial progress in reasoning, systematically producing frontier-level reasoning data remains difficult. Existing synthesis methods often have limited visibility into the structural factors that govern problem difficulty, which can result in narrow diversity and unstable difficulty control. In this work, we view the difficulty of a reasoning problem as arising from the accumulation of atomic knowledge-reasoning transformations, which we term thought modes. Building on this perspective, we propose MindLoom, a framework for synthesizing frontier-level reasoning data through compositional thought mode engineering. Given a collection of hard problems with verified solutions, MindLoom first decomposes those solutions into thought mode chains that reveal each problem's construction logic. It then trains a retrieval model that matches problem states to compatible thought modes, providing guidance on which reasoning challenges to introduce during synthesis. New problems are composed by iteratively applying retrieved thought modes to seed questions, with distribution-aligned sampling to encourage diverse reasoning coverage. Finally, a rollout-based judging stage labels generated questions by difficulty and supplies judged-correct responses for supervised fine-tuning. We evaluate MindLoom on nine benchmarks covering five STEM disciplines and four mathematical reasoning tasks across multiple model families and sizes. Models fine-tuned on MindLoom-generated data achieves favorable performances over base models, distillation, and external-data baselines across the reported benchmarks. Ablation studies indicate the contribution of each component, and further analysis suggests that MindLoom covers a broad range of reasoning patterns while maintaining useful difficulty control. We have open-sourced our implementation at https://github.com/EachSheep/MindLoom.
2605.21630.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在推理方面取得了顯著進展，但系統性地生成前沿水平的推理數據仍然困難。現有的合成方法往往對於影響問題難度的結構性因素可見度有限，這可能導致多樣性狹窄和難度控制不穩定。在這項工作中，我們將推理問題的難度視為原子知識-推理轉換的累積，這些轉換我們稱之為思維模式。基於這一觀點，我們提出了MindLoom，一個通過組合思維模式工程來合成前沿水平推理數據的框架。給定一組具有驗證解決方案的困難問題，MindLoom首先將這些解決方案分解為揭示每個問題構建邏輯的思維模式鏈。然後，它訓練一個檢索模型，將問題狀態與兼容的思維模式匹配，提供在合成過程中引入哪些推理挑戰的指導。新問題通過迭代應用檢索到的思維模式於種子問題來組成，並進行分佈對齊的抽樣以促進多樣化的推理覆蓋。最後，一個基於回滾的評判階段根據難度標記生成的問題，並提供經評判正確的回答以進行監督微調。我們在涵蓋五個STEM學科和四個數學推理任務的九個基準上評估了MindLoom，涉及多個模型系列和大小。在報告的基準上，基於MindLoom生成的數據微調的模型在性能上優於基線模型、蒸餾和外部數據基準。消融研究表明了每個組件的貢獻，進一步分析顯示MindLoom涵蓋了廣泛的推理模式，同時保持了有用的難度控制。我們已在https://github.com/EachSheep/MindLoom開源了我們的實現。
2605.21630.author: Haiyang Shen et.al.
2605.21630.authors: Haiyang Shen, Taian Guo, Xuanzhong Chen, Mugeng Liu, Weichen Bi, Wenchun Jing, Sixiong Xie, Zhuofan Shi, Yudong Han, Chongyang Pan, Siqi Zhong, Jinsheng Huang, Ming Zhang, Yun Ma
